observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Ensure strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances by issuing a memorandum reiterating compliance by all officers and employees of the Municipality; b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold payment of salary and other money due and/or appropriate sanction as stated in the regulations to the accountable officers of the Municipality who failed to comply.

We recommended that Management: a. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the DA-BFAR Region 10 thru the Municipal Agriculture Officer, in facilitating the submission of the documents related to the Watch Tower project; b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer on the status of project recorded under the CIP account and reclassify it to its appropriate PPE accounts duly supported with its Certificate of Project Completion.

We recommend that Management: a. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit the complete documentation of the turnover of the MPB projects from DPWH to the Municipal Accountant. b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting journal entries to: • Recognize the assets after receipt of the necessary documents from the Engineering Office and reclassify the attributable expenses incorrectly recorded in CIP account and record them as additions to the main asset. • Recognize and record the corresponding depreciation and accumulated depreciation.

We recommended that Management should: a. Prepare an updated PIP for the ensuing inventory taking and include the extraction of data per office/department for a more organized actual inventory taking. b. Expedite the submission of a COMPLETE Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE)/Physical Inventory Report as of December 31, 2024. c. Schedule the reconciliation of the results of the inventory count to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in the respective records of the Property and Accounting Units. d. Review section 7 to 8 of COA Circular 2020-006 on the procedural guidelines on the disposition and derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs. e. Avail and/or begin the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Instruct the MBO to devise a strategy in order that the Municipality be able to set-up or allocate necessary funds for terminal leave benefits by accruing the actual earned leave benefits of all officials and employees of the Municipality, including those of the members of the Sangguniang Bayan; b. Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to take up as one of the adjusting entries at year-end the accrued liability, and include a disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statement the details of such amount based on the updated and corrected leave credit balances and in accordance with IPSAS 19.

We recommended that Management limit the payroll deductions to the order of preference provided in the GAA and should ensure that the approval of loan applications of personnel should not cause their net take home pay to be below P5,000 as required by the GAA.

We recommended that Management prioritize the updating of the official website of the Municipality with information mentioned in Sections 97 and 98 of GAA 2024 and maintain yearly the update of this information by assigning web administrators for these tasks.

We recommended that Management should: a. Expedite the submission of a COMPLETE Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE)/Physical Inventory Report as of December 31, 2023. b. Schedule the reconciliation of the results of the inventory count to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in the respective records of the Property and Accounting Units. c. Review section 7 to 8 of COA Circular 2020-006 on the procedural guidelines on the disposition and derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to: i. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect revaluation model using the fair market value provided by the Municipal Assessor in accordance with IPSAS 17; ii. Enjoin the Municipal Assessor to: a. Exhaust all possible means to facilitate the processing on the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the Municipal Government of Laguindingan with due consideration to the memorandum of encumbrance in compliance to COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and b. Seek budgetary allocations for the pertinent cost thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Officer to monitor the extent of the implementation of the LDRRMFIP by coordinating with the members of the BAC for the proper scheduling of procurements for PPAs included in the LDRRMFIP to ensure that the public receive the benefits that may accrue from the utilization of the fund. We also recommended that the LDRRMFIP be presented in such a way that specific details could be directly attributed with the PPAs initially presented in the Annual Budget and avoid presenting them in lump-sum amounts in order to easily monitor its utilization. We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to ensure that purchases of relief and disaster-related items, supplies and materials are recorded under the appropriate Inventory accounts and recognize the appropriate expense accounts only upon its issuance.

We recommended the Management to impose the following: 1. The Municipal Treasurer to render her monthly reports such as Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) with supporting documents, Payrolls, Official Receipts and other liquidation documents including revenue reports and supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant on or before the 5th day of the ensuing month to allow its prompt recording in the books of accounts. 2. The Municipal Accountant to faithfully comply and enforce the periodic submission of these reports within the prescribed period. 3. Concerned officials shall prioritize and adopt measures to address issues on delayed submission of reports to be able to meet the deadline set and be free from possible sanctions that may be imposed from violating thereof, specifically the suspension of payment of salaries. 4. Relieve the Municipal Accountant of some other ministerial duties and assign them to other municipal officials, especially when reporting period is approaching.

We recommended to management to include in the CY 2023 municipal budget the payment of insurance premium in compliance to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated September 12, 2012 and to insure with the GSIS the Municipality’s insurable assets and properties to reduce or cope with the potential negative consequences of disasters in the future.

We recommended that Management undertake the following: Require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the 5% share of DPWH Secretary to their Special Account No. 154 thru the Bureau of Treasury. She shall notify the DPWH Secretary thru the NBCDO that its 5% share has already been remitted to his account.

We recommended that the management undertake the following: • Require the Municipal Agriculturist to submit the MOA between the LGU and the people’s organization. • The Municipal Accountant should issue demand letter to the grantee requiring liquidation of the funds transferred.

We recommended that the Management should undertake the following: a. Require the MEEDO Manager to prepare a business plan for each of the LGU’s Economic Enterprise in accordance with Appendix A of LBC 111. b. Require the LFC to conduct Semi-Annual Financial Self-Sufficiency Review rather than wait for the BPs to be prepared or revised. c. Prepare a separate financial report for each LEE and attribute costs to each LEE in accordance with Section 6.6.3 (b) of LBC No. 111 so that financial report for each LEE can be generated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Engineer to provide the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to the Accounting and GSO especially for projects prior to the year 2024. The GSO should prepare the Report of the Local Road Networks and the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Networks as prescribed in Annex A and C of COA Circular 2015-008. The Municipal Accountant should maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for the road and its components namely a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures and d) other miscellaneous structures and compute depreciation separately for road components taking into consideration its useful life and initial cost. We further recommended that the accounting and reporting procedures and guidelines set forth in COA Circular 2015-008 be strictly followed, the required reports be submitted to this office and the road networks be properly disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the Agency.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to: 1. Dig up records available in the Accounting Office and conduct verification, analysis, and validation of the receivable accounts; 2. Determine if the accounts could already be classified as dormant accounts in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005; 3. Exhaust all possible remedies by the management to collect the receivables and to demand liquidation of cash advances and fund transfers. If it is still impractical to do so due to lack or absence of records, death or difficulty in locating the debtors, file the request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommend that Management should: 1. Decide whether or not to permanently takeover the operation of the Laguindingan Water District; 2. Demand from the then governing board of the Laguindingan Water District inventory of their assets and liabilities, their financial records which includes creditors and consumers. Should the LGU decide to permanently takeover the LWD, the loan agreement between LWD and LWUA must also be turned over; 3. Require the then governing board of the LWD to submit proof of compliance with Section 44 of PD 198; 4. Coordinate with LWUA regarding the unilateral assumption of the Laguindingan Water District; and 5. Reflect proper accounting in the books of LGU should the LGU decide to permanently take over the LWD. The water district should be recognized as an economic enterprise of the LGU pursuant to Section 105 of the NGAS Manual and separate financial statements must also be prepared.

We recommended that: a. The Municipal Mayor should require the GSO, Engineering, Accounting conduct of an inventory of the local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction and the reconciliation of the list of inventories per local road inventory report and road map with those recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructure (RPI). b. The Local Chief Executive should direct the General Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the road map of the Municipality to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the Municipality’s road network system. c. The Municipal Engineer should provide the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to the Municipal Accountant and the GSO. d. Separate subsidiary ledgers must be maintained for the road and its components namely a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures and d) other miscellaneous structures and compute depreciation separately for road components. We further recommend that the accounting and reporting procedures and guidelines set forth in COA Circular 2015-008 be strictly followed, the required reports be submitted to this office and the road networks be properly disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the Agency.
We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the Liquidation Reports and to ensure timely submission for subsequent periods.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management should:
a. Expedite the submission of a COMPLETE Report on Physical Count of PPE
(RPCPPE)/Physical Inventory Report as of December 31, 2023.
b. Schedule the reconciliation of the results of the inventory count to ensure that all
PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in the respective records of the
Property and Accounting Units.
c. Review section 7 to 8 of COA Circular 2020-006 on the procedural
guidelines on the disposition and derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs.

Recommendation:
We recommended and management is amenable to direct the Municipal Accountant
to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer on the status of projects recorded under the CIP
accounts and reclassify the completed projects to its appropriate PPE accounts duly
supported with Certificates of Project Completion. Completed projects recorded under the
Trust Fund shall be transferred to the General Fund upon submission of full liquidation to
the grantor. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant shall promptly reclassify projects
recorded under the CIP to its appropriate PPE account upon completion of the project.

Recommendations:
We recommended and for which the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
1. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect revaluation model using the fair market
value provided by the Municipal Assessor in accordance with IPSAS 17;
2. Enjoin the Municipal Assessor to:
a. Exhaust all possible means to facilitate the processing on the legality
of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT)
in the name of the Municipal Government of Laguindingan with due
consideration to the memorandum of encumbrance in compliance to
COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and
b. Seek budgetary allocations for the pertinent cost thereof.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and the Local Finance
Committee (LFC) to ensure that the projects to be funded by the LDF, especially
thru re-alignment, are derived from the local development plans and that PPAs
are included in the AIP. In case all PPAs included in the AIP were already
funded, a supplemental AIP should be prepared which will be the basis in
funding additional PPAs and shall be submitted to COA.
b. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Budget
Officer to make sure that all programs and activities that are to be implemented
by the Municipality under the 20% DF should contain details as to the amount,
name, nature and place to be undertaken. Lump sum appropriation for PPAs
should be discouraged.
c. Enjoin the Municipal Engineer, MPDC and MDC including the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) in planning and scheduling all activities pertaining
to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and
completion. Consider priority projects that are feasible, giving emphasis in
spurring development, providing jobs to constituents and achieving the desired
socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU so that
the intended users/beneficiaries may enjoy the immediate benefits therefrom.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor is agreeable to direct the Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction Officer to monitor the extent of the implementation of the
LDRRMFIP by coordinating with the members of the BAC for the proper scheduling of
procurements for PPAs included in the LDRRMFIP to ensure that the public receive the
benefits that may accrue from the utilization of the fund.

We also recommended and for which the Municipal Mayor also agreed that the
LDRRMFIP be presented in such a way that specific details could be directly attributed
with the PPAs initially presented in the Annual Budget and avoid presenting them in lump-
sum amounts in order to easily monitor its utilization.

Procurement of several disaster-related items and other supplies and inventories
totaling P0.101 million were recorded as outright expenses, instead of an appropriate
Inventory account, contrary to COA Circular No. 2014- 002, resulting in the
misstatement of the inventory and expense accounts by the same amount.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor concurred to ensure that purchases
of relief and disaster-related items, supplies and materials are recorded under the
appropriate Inventory accounts and recognize the appropriate expense accounts only upon
its issuance.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to impose the following:
1. The Municipal Treasurer to render her monthly reports such as Disbursement
Vouchers (DVs) with supporting documents, Payrolls, Official Receipts and
other liquidation documents including revenue reports and supporting
documents to the Municipal Accountant on or before the 5th day of the ensuing
month to allow its prompt recording in the books of accounts.
2. The Municipal Accountant to faithfully comply and enforce the periodic
submission of these reports within the prescribed period. Concerned officials
shall likewise prioritize submission of these reports and look into the root cause
of these delays and immediately initiate measures to address the problem.
3. Concerned officials shall prioritize and adopt measures to address issues on
delayed submission of reports to be able to meet the deadline set and be free
from possible sanctions that may be imposed from violating thereof,
specifically the suspension of payment of salaries.
4. Relieve the Municipal Accountant of some other ministerial duties and assign
them to other municipal officials, especially when reporting period is
approaching.

Submit immediately the approved budget or the realignment of the 20% development fund used as COVID-19 response fund. Also submit the revised or updated Annual Investment Plan for the changes/realignment of the 20% Development Fund.
Submit a list of recipients of the food packs prepared by the LGU indicating the quantity of rice and other food items each household received.
The BAC should avoid filling in details in the item description portion of the Requests for Quotation and also encourage suppliers to put the brand names of items quoted. Also, the management should accomplish properly the purchase orders including the brand names and other detailed specifications of goods to be procured in order to serve the purpose for which the purchase order was prescribed.
Require the BAC to submit an explanation as to why projects were completed ahead of the purchase orders and why a cash advance was granted to pay for the four infrastructure projects?
Require the MTO-ICO to submit an explanation why the infrastructure projects were paid out of his cash advance? Likewise, the BAC to submit an explanation as to why the projects were started without a valid contract for the supply of materials?
Lastly, the management should justify the purchase of CCTV Camera out of the MTO-ICO’s cash advance charged against the 20% development fund. The MTO-ICO should refund the ₱700.00 used to pay for home card membership fee.

We have recommended to the management to:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list of taxpayers with corresponding amount of tax due and collectible for the year which should be the basis of the accounting to set-up RPT/SET Receivable at the beginning of the year;
Require the Municipal Assessor to submit to the Municipal Treasurer on or before the 31st day of every December a list which contains all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties.
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to prepare journal entries to adjust the RPT/SET receivables account to reflect their correct balances in the LGU’s books.

We have recommended and the Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant with copies of the report on completed projects (e.g. certificates of completion and inspection reports) after validation on the status of the said projects. Also, the same shall assign a project ID unique for every project for management and monitoring purposes.
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to conduct periodic reconciliation of records to minimize differences in the report and ensure the timely recognition of assets.

Demand immediate return or liquidation of the cash advances amounting to ₱679,296.91 and/or cause the withholding of the amount due from the salaries of the concerned officials and employees should they fail to liquidate and/or return unused funds.
Do not grant additional or new cash advance to special disbursing officers unless the outstanding cash advance is settled first.
Strictly comply with Sections 5.7 and 5.8 of COA Circular 97-002.

We have recommended and the management agreed to:
Direct the collectors to turn over/remit all collections daily and intact to the Liquidating Officer in compliance with Sections 26 and 31 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
Require the Municipal Treasurer, Liquidating Officer, and collectors to record collections and remittances daily in the cashbooks to enable them to reconcile cashbook balance with the RCD/RCR balance and cash on hand to ensure the accuracy of their reports.
Enjoin the Liquidating Officer to check the accuracy of reports submitted by the collectors before remitting cash to the Municipal Treasurer.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit collections intact and daily. If daily deposit is not possible due to the early cut-off time of the bank during this pandemic and due to the proximity of the LGU to the depository banks, a deposit of three (3) times a week should be achieved.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group should meticulously review all items in the program of works and check conformity with existing rules and regulations to avoid any excess in contract cost.
We also required submission of satisfactory explanation and/or justification for the computed excess of contract cost over COA evaluated cost and submit back up computation for Item IV, clearing and grubbing of the four contracts mentioned.

Recommendation
We recommended that the management should notify the contractor of the deficiencies noted and direct the latter to immediately rectify the defects to prevent further damage otherwise the equivalent amount of the defects noted shall be disallowed in audit.
Compliance report shall be made and submitted to this office accompanied by detailed report on the works done and pictures.
It is further recommended that management should ensure that project billboards contain all necessary information regarding the project and that no details should be left blank.

We recommend that the LCE should organize/reactivate the project monitoring committee. And when established, they should conduct thorough evaluation and/or inspection of actual accomplishments of all projects implemented by the Municipality.
We also require the accomplishment of the variance noted during inspection and the unimplemented 30 meters road shouldering, otherwise it shall be disallowed in audit.
We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries if the road shouldering were recorded as completed projects.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant should review the Other Payables balances in each fund and provide details of transactions; 







The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer and respective departments whether they are still going to implement the obligated programs and activities, otherwise necessary adjusting entries should be made and the said funds should be declared as part of the LGU’s savings and be reprogrammed; and

The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant should obligate programs and activities and record transactions in accordance with the principle of accrual accounting to reflect fairly the effects of transactions.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2019 | The Other Payables account has already been reviewed using the documents available for periods as far as CY 2007. Amounts verified were reclassified to their appropriate Accounts Payable or Trust Liability accounts with corresponding Subsidiary Ledgers. The amounts traced from prior years AARs that have been in the books for more than ten years were already earmarked for write off adhering to applicable rules and regulations.  Adjustments were made to remove stale checks and obligations for programs that would no longer be implemented especially those that should have been charged to the Special Education Fund.

Most of the obligations from prior years are still for implementation and were perpetually delayed due to the changes brought by the pandemic.

Obligated transactions, programs and activities at the end of the year strictly follows the accrual principle and a detailed list is maintained by both the Accounting and Budget Office for the monitoring of its implementation in the succeeding year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Agriculturist as to the existence and whereabouts of the Breeding Stocks and if possible, secure documents related to the said accounts. Moreover, prepare and maintain the Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Ledger Card (WOABSLC) as provided under Sections 114 of the NGAS Manual; and






The GSO Designate should include in the conduct of physical inventory the breeding stocks/animals in coordination with the Municipal Agriculturist to facilitate documentation of the breeding stocks inventory.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2019 | Both the Accounting Office and the Municipal Agriculture Office reviewed its records as far back as 2008 – 2009 to trace the recorded Breeding Stocks. 

There are no supporting documents of the recorded Breeding S tocks available in the Agriculture Office. 

Based on the discussion of the two depts., the LGU inappropriately recorded the breeding stocks turned over to qualified recipients during several dispersals on 2009 – 2011 as Breeding Stocks of the LGU.

In the latest inventory report of the GSO, it was indicated that the LGU has no Breeding Stocks inventory.
observations.reason: The breeding stocks recorded were not yet written off pending the submission of the report from GSO to the Accounting Office on the physical count of inventories.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
         We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Engineer to provide the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to the Municipal Accountant so that the latter may record them separately in the ledger cards of each Road Network. 


	The GSO should prepare the Report of the Local Road Networks and the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Networks as prescribed in Annex A and C of COA Circular 2015-008. Also, the GSO should maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components (see Annex E of the said circular).





	The Municipal Accountant should maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for the road and its components namely a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures and d) other miscellaneous structures (see Annex D of COA Circular 2015-008). These ledgers shall be kept in the Accounting division and shall be maintained for each local road. Depreciation shall be computed separately for road components taking into consideration its useful life and initial cost. 




	We further recommended that the accounting and reporting procedures and guidelines set forth in COA Circular 2015-008 be strictly followed, the required reports be submitted to this office and the road networks be properly disclosed in the Notes to the |Financial Statements of the Agency in accordance with Annex B of the said circular.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2019 | The LCE already directed the Engineering Office to assist both the GSO and Accounting Office in the LGU’s efforts to comply the requirements of COA Circular 2015-008. In fact, the LGU hired additional engineers tasked to focus on the Local Road Assets Management System.




The Municipal Accountant already recognize in the books various Infrastructure Assets (Road Networks, Water Supply Systems, Other Infrastructure Assets) and set up their corresponding subsidiary ledgers (per project completed). The subsidiary ledgers will be updated upon finalization of the Engineering Office’s detailed component report of each road network and other infrastructure assets identified.

The Infrastructure Asset accounts like Road Networks, Water Supply Systems and Other Infrastructure Assets are also properly disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Engineering Office is still in the process of completing the description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.




Not Implemented

The GSO have not yet prepared the Report of the Local Road Networks and the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Networks and have not yet maintained a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and have not yet kept a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. 

Partially Implemented

The recording is not yet complete for the Engineering Dept. has yet to complete the details and cost segregation of roads and their components.




Partially Implemented 

There are still roads and components for recording under Road Networks pending the submission of details and cost from the Engineering Office.

Separate depreciation for each road component is not yet implemented.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations

     The Management should:

Ensure that program flow or activity matrix are prepared for the preparedness program.





Adhere strictly with the provisions and guidelines set forth under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No.  2013-1 and utilize the LDRRMF only for the specific purpose for which it has been appropriated, particularly programs and activities that supports pre-disaster and disaster risk management as mandated by R.A. No. 10121.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2019 | The MDRRMO has prepared a detailed activity matrix particularly for its Preparedness Program coinciding with its Investment Plan and even with its Annual Procurement Plan.  

The disaster risk management programs are regularly reviewed by the local DRRM council to ensure compliance to guidelines and as well as relevance and applicability to the current needs of the LGU.

AAR CY 2019 | The Municipal Engineer sent letters to the contractors and requested for the rectification of defects. Consequently, contractors removed and replaced the said defects.

The Municipal Engineer submitted compliance report, with photos attached, on the rectifications made by the contractors with

An inspection team formed was tasked to inspect on-going projects and give comments on the implementation of these projects.

The Engineering Office

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation

We recommended that Management should not process advance payment to contractors without a letter request from the contractor requesting advance payment. The contractor must be obliged to submit an irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand, issued by a surety or insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance Commission and confirmed by the procuring entity before payment of mobilization or advance payment. The Municipal Engineer should require such contract documents from the contractors.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2019 | All contractors were required to submit irrevocable standby letter of credit or surety bond before any advance payments were made.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation

      We recommended that the Municipal Engineer should require the concerned contractors for each completed project which have already been fully paid as enumerated in Annex A to post or submit the warranty security for their respective projects.

	We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should ensure that warranty security be posted before final payment of projects in accordance with Section 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular 2012-001.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2019 | The Municipal Engineer strictly imposed the submission of warranty security.


The Municipal Accountant ensures that warranty security is posted as prerequisite for the final billing.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the Municipal Engineer to:

Strictly comply with the guidelines set forth under Section 9 and 10 of Annex E of the IRR of RA 9184 in granting requests for suspension of work or time extensions. Evaluate requests for time extension and examine the facts and extent of the delay before granting time extensions;



Provide a detailed report on the time extensions given to contractors as enumerated in Annex A of this AOM; and



Submit the necessary documents as required under Annex E of COA Circular 2009-001 pertaining to the evaluation of time extension to determine the reasonableness of the grounds for the issuance and period of the requested extension of contract time.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2019 | The Engineering Office strictly complies with the guidelines in granting the request for suspension of work and time extensions to determine reasonableness of the grounds for the issuance and period of extensions. 

A detailed report was submitted on the time extensions given to contractors. 

The Engineering Office was able to determine the reasonableness of the grounds for the issuance of suspensions or time extensions. If the reason/s for the delay is/are not acceptable or if the projects are not completed on time, liquidated damages will be computed and collected.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the BAC Members and end users to:

Avoid “tailor-fit” procurement. Reference to brand names or circumventing it by enumerating specifications which point out to a specific brand should be discouraged because it defeats the very essence of competitive bidding. Procurement of goods shall be based on relevant characteristics and/or performance requirements.

Submit justification on why the specifications indicated in the procurement request of the subject procurement was the same as that of ford raptor.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2019 | The BAC was already directed by the LCE to ensure that the basis for the procurement of goods as well as services must be on performance requirements and other important characteristics.

Justification on the specifications of the procurement request was already submitted.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

    We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer should ensure that all documentary requirements were submitted by the contractor and attached to the vouchers before payment thereof is authorized.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2019 | A detailed checklist is provided and adhered to by the Engineering department before submitting documentary requirements to the Accounting and Budget Office for processing of payments.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations

The Management should:

Provide an explanation on the abnormal balances of line items in the SAAOB as of December 31, 2019. The Municipal Budget Officer should monitor and reconcile the Budget vis-à-vis the actual amounts on a regular basis to avoid incurring obligation in excess of the budget.

Compel the Local Finance Committee and the Municipal Accountant to accomplish the required quarterly reports (i.e. Quarterly Financial Reports) and reconcile differences if there are any.




Ensure that the required provisions and activities are well-planned to avoid negative variances. 




Require the department heads to monitor the status of implementation of their respective Programs, Projects, and Activities (PPAs) and should be able to determine the reason for variances and propose actions to address such variances.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2019 | The Budget Officer already submitted an explanation regarding the abnormal balances.


Strict monitoring is now being imposed in the automated system of SAAOB or RAO to avoid recurrence of negative balances.

The reconstituted LFC conducts regular meetings to review and reconcile financial data. Additional manpower was also hired for the Treasury, Budget, and Accounting Offices to assist in the preparation of all the necessary financial reports.

The Management is now more conscious in planning well the activities and financial requirements to avoid negative variances.

The heads of offices have also been constantly reminded to monitor the implementation of their programs and projects and reconcile regularly with the Budget Officer regarding the remaining balances of their appropriations.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations

The Management should:

Limit obligations for transactions such as payments to job orders to the existing allotment and appropriation. Changes to the budget should be supported by ordinances and supplemental budget. 




Discuss the kind of job order services needed per department before the formulation of the budget. This includes the applicable skills, the number of people to do the job, and the duration of the services. 




Classify the payments to job order workers to its appropriate account.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2019 | Appropriations for payments to job orders are charged to existing special purpose appropriations in their assigned offices. Budget supplementation or realignment are all supported with ordinance.

To address the manpower issues and handle human relations functions, the LGU hired additional regular employee in the HR department. Hiring the right people and determining the appropriate number of people for the job are among of her core duties.

In the financial statements, appropriate account codes were used per fund to record the services provided by these job orders in their respective assignments.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations

The Management should:

Ensure that all claims should be supported with legal basis (i.e. applicable laws, ordinance, executive order).



Process claims only when supporting documents are complete.



Evaluate whether all MEEDO and MTO employees are required to be present every Market Day for collection purposes. Otherwise, the concerned department heads should instead make a schedule requiring employees to report only on specific dates.

The Local Chief Executive should direct the General Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the road map of the Municipality to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the Municipality’s road network system.

Separate subsidiary ledgers must be maintained for the road and its components namely a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures and d) other miscellaneous structures. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Mayor should require the conduct of an inventory of the local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction and the reconciliation of the list of inventory per local road inventory report and road map with those recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructure (RPI).

The Local Chief Executive should direct the General Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the road map of the Municipality to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the Municipality’s road network system.

The Municipal Engineer should provide the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to the Municipal Accountant and the GSO. This information is needed in the required reports under COA Circular 2015-008.

Separate subsidiary ledgers must be maintained for the road and its components namely a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures and d) other miscellaneous structures. These ledgers shall be kept in the Accounting division and shall be maintained for each local road.

We further recommend that the accounting and reporting procedures and guidelines set forth in COA Circular 2015-008 be strictly followed, the required reports be submitted to this office and the road networks be properly disclosed in the Notes to the |Financial Statements of the Agency.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant should review the Other Payables balances in each fund and provide details of transactions;
The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer and respective departments whether they are still going to implement the obligated programs and activities, otherwise necessary adjusting entries should be made and the said funds should be declared as part of the LGU’s savings and be reprogrammed; and
The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant should obligate programs and activities and record transactions in accordance with the principle of accrual accounting to reflect fairly the effects of transactions.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Agriculturist as to the existence and whereabouts of the Breeding Stocks and if possible, secure documents related to the said accounts. Moreover, prepare and maintain the Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Ledger Card (WOABSLC) as provided under Sections 114 of the NGAS Manual; and
The GSO Designate should include in the conduct of physical inventory the breeding stocks/animals in coordination with the Municipal Agriculturist to facilitate documentation of the breeding stocks inventory.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Engineer to provide the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to the Municipal Accountant so that the latter may record them separately in the ledger cards of each Road Network.
The GSO should prepare the Report of the Local Road Networks and the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Networks as prescribed in Annex A and C of COA Circular 2015-008. Also, the GSO should maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components (see Annex E of the said circular).
The Municipal Accountant should maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for the road and its components namely a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures and d) other miscellaneous structures (see Annex D of COA Circular 2015-008). These ledgers shall be kept in the Accounting division and shall be maintained for each local road. Depreciation shall be computed separately for road components taking into consideration its useful life and initial cost.
We further recommended that the accounting and reporting procedures and guidelines set forth in COA Circular 2015-008 be strictly followed, the required reports be submitted to this office and the road networks be properly disclosed in the Notes to the |Financial Statements of the Agency in accordance with Annex B of the said circular.

Recommendations
The Management should:
Ensure that program flow or activity matrix are prepared for the preparedness program.
Adhere strictly with the provisions and guidelines set forth under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No.  2013-1 and utilize the LDRRMF only for the specific purpose for which it has been appropriated, particularly programs and activities that supports pre-disaster and disaster risk management as mandated by R.A. No. 10121.

Recommendation
We recommended that Management should not process advance payment to contractors without a letter request from the contractor requesting advance payment. The contractor must be obliged to submit an irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand, issued by a surety or insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance Commission and confirmed by the procuring entity before payment of mobilization or advance payment. The Municipal Engineer should require such contract documents from the contractors.

Recommendation
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer should require the concerned contractors for each completed project which have already been fully paid as enumerated in Annex A to post or submit the warranty security for their respective projects.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should ensure that warranty security be posted before final payment of projects in accordance with Section 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular 2012-001.

Recommendations
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the Municipal Engineer to:
Strictly comply with the guidelines set forth under Section 9 and 10 of Annex E of the IRR of RA 9184 in granting requests for suspension of work or time extensions. Evaluate requests for time extension and examine the facts and extent of the delay before granting time extensions;
Provide a detailed report on the time extensions given to contractors as enumerated in Annex A of this AOM; and
Submit the necessary documents as required under Annex E of COA Circular 2009-001 pertaining to the evaluation of time extension to determine the reasonableness of the grounds for the issuance and period of the requested extension of contract time.

Recommendation
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the BAC Members and end users to:
Avoid “tailor-fit” procurement. Reference to brand names or circumventing it by enumerating specifications which point out to a specific brand should be discouraged because it defeats the very essence of competitive bidding. Procurement of goods shall be based on relevant characteristics and/or performance requirements.
Submit justification on why the specifications indicated in the procurement request of the subject procurement was the same as that of ford raptor.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer should ensure that all documentary requirements were submitted by the contractor and attached to the vouchers before payment thereof is authorized.

Recommendations
The Management should:
Provide an explanation on the abnormal balances of line items in the SAAOB as of December 31, 2019. The Municipal Budget Officer should monitor and reconcile the Budget vis-à-vis the actual amounts on a regular basis to avoid incurring obligation in excess of the budget.
Compel the Local Finance Committee and the Municipal Accountant to accomplish the required quarterly reports (i.e. Quarterly Financial Reports) and reconcile differences if there are any.
Ensure that the required provisions and activities are well-planned to avoid negative variances.
Require the department heads to monitor the status of implementation of their respective Programs, Projects, and Activities (PPAs) and should be able to determine the reason for variances and propose actions to address such variances.

Recommendations
The Management should:
Limit obligations for transactions such as payments to job orders to the existing allotment and appropriation. Changes to the budget should be supported by ordinances and supplemental budget.
Discuss the kind of job order services needed per department before the formulation of the budget. This includes the applicable skills, the number of people to do the job, and the duration of the services.
Classify the payments to job order workers to its appropriate account.

Recommendations
The Management should:
Ensure that all claims should be supported with legal basis (i.e. applicable laws, ordinance, executive order).
Process claims only when supporting documents are complete.
Evaluate whether all MEEDO and MTO employees are required to be present every Market Day for collection purposes. Otherwise, the concerned department heads should instead make a schedule requiring employees to report only on specific dates.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant should review the Other Payables balances in each fund and provide details of transactions;
The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer and respective departments whether they are still going to implement the obligated programs and activities, otherwise necessary adjusting entries should be made and the said funds should be declared as part of the LGU’s savings and be reprogrammed; and
The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant should obligate programs and activities and record transactions in accordance with the principle of accrual accounting to reflect fairly the effects of transactions.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Agriculturist as to the existence and whereabouts of the Breeding Stocks and if possible, secure documents related to the said accounts. Moreover, prepare and maintain the Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Ledger Card (WOABSLC) as provided under Sections 114 of the NGAS Manual; and
The GSO Designate should include in the conduct of physical inventory the breeding stocks/animals in coordination with the Municipal Agriculturist to facilitate documentation of the breeding stocks inventory.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Engineer to provide the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to the Municipal Accountant so that the latter may record them separately in the ledger cards of each Road Network.
The GSO should prepare the Report of the Local Road Networks and the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Networks as prescribed in Annex A and C of COA Circular 2015-008. Also, the GSO should maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components (see Annex E of the said circular).
The Municipal Accountant should maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for the road and its components namely a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures and d) other miscellaneous structures (see Annex D of COA Circular 2015-008). These ledgers shall be kept in the Accounting division and shall be maintained for each local road. Depreciation shall be computed separately for road components taking into consideration its useful life and initial cost.
We further recommended that the accounting and reporting procedures and guidelines set forth in COA Circular 2015-008 be strictly followed, the required reports be submitted to this office and the road networks be properly disclosed in the Notes to the |Financial Statements of the Agency in accordance with Annex B of the said circular.

Recommendations
The Management should:
Ensure that program flow or activity matrix are prepared for the preparedness program.
Adhere strictly with the provisions and guidelines set forth under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No.  2013-1 and utilize the LDRRMF only for the specific purpose for which it has been appropriated, particularly programs and activities that supports pre-disaster and disaster risk management as mandated by R.A. No. 10121.

Recommendation
We recommended that Management should not process advance payment to contractors without a letter request from the contractor requesting advance payment. The contractor must be obliged to submit an irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand, issued by a surety or insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance Commission and confirmed by the procuring entity before payment of mobilization or advance payment. The Municipal Engineer should require such contract documents from the contractors.

Recommendation
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer should require the concerned contractors for each completed project which have already been fully paid as enumerated in Annex A to post or submit the warranty security for their respective projects.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should ensure that warranty security be posted before final payment of projects in accordance with Section 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular 2012-001.

Recommendations
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the Municipal Engineer to:
Strictly comply with the guidelines set forth under Section 9 and 10 of Annex E of the IRR of RA 9184 in granting requests for suspension of work or time extensions. Evaluate requests for time extension and examine the facts and extent of the delay before granting time extensions;
Provide a detailed report on the time extensions given to contractors as enumerated in Annex A of this AOM; and
Submit the necessary documents as required under Annex E of COA Circular 2009-001 pertaining to the evaluation of time extension to determine the reasonableness of the grounds for the issuance and period of the requested extension of contract time.

Recommendation
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the BAC Members and end users to:
Avoid “tailor-fit” procurement. Reference to brand names or circumventing it by enumerating specifications which point out to a specific brand should be discouraged because it defeats the very essence of competitive bidding. Procurement of goods shall be based on relevant characteristics and/or performance requirements.
Submit justification on why the specifications indicated in the procurement request of the subject procurement was the same as that of ford raptor.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer should ensure that all documentary requirements were submitted by the contractor and attached to the vouchers before payment thereof is authorized.

Recommendations
The Management should:
Provide an explanation on the abnormal balances of line items in the SAAOB as of December 31, 2019. The Municipal Budget Officer should monitor and reconcile the Budget vis-à-vis the actual amounts on a regular basis to avoid incurring obligation in excess of the budget.
Compel the Local Finance Committee and the Municipal Accountant to accomplish the required quarterly reports (i.e. Quarterly Financial Reports) and reconcile differences if there are any.
Ensure that the required provisions and activities are well-planned to avoid negative variances.
Require the department heads to monitor the status of implementation of their respective Programs, Projects, and Activities (PPAs) and should be able to determine the reason for variances and propose actions to address such variances.

Recommendations
The Management should:
Limit obligations for transactions such as payments to job orders to the existing allotment and appropriation. Changes to the budget should be supported by ordinances and supplemental budget.
Discuss the kind of job order services needed per department before the formulation of the budget. This includes the applicable skills, the number of people to do the job, and the duration of the services.
Classify the payments to job order workers to its appropriate account.

Recommendations
The Management should:
Ensure that all claims should be supported with legal basis (i.e. applicable laws, ordinance, executive order).
Process claims only when supporting documents are complete.
Evaluate whether all MEEDO and MTO employees are required to be present every Market Day for collection purposes. Otherwise, the concerned department heads should instead make a schedule requiring employees to report only on specific dates.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The Management should create an inventory committee that would conduct the physical count and would prepare the complete Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.
Require the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to strictly adhere to existing rules and regulations on the maintenance of PPELC and PC and ensure the conduct of the required annual physical count of PPE and reconciliation with the Accounting and GSO records against the related PPELC and PC to facilitate timely verification and adjustment of any discrepancies noted.

We recommended that the management:
Implement the accrual basis of recognizing revenue to conform with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards.
Ensure that all market stall lessee shall have lease contracts and that the prescribed rates in the Municipal Ordinance No. 273 s. 2017 shall be implemented accordingly.

We recommended that:
Additional staff should be provided to the accounting office.
The Municipal Accountant should submit the aforementioned reports and vouchers immediately.
The Municipal Accountant should prioritize the submission of the monthly trial balances, financial statements, disbursement vouchers, liquidation reports, and Bank Reconciliation Statements and should ensure that these are submitted on or before the reglementary period set by the government.
We recommended and management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the Liquidation Reports and to ensure timely submission for subsequent periods.

We recommended the following:
The Management shall release from the OIC – Municipal Treasurer the function of disbursing officer to give priority in intensifying the collection of revenue and other regular duties/reports.
Designate liquidating officer from among the collecting officers so that collections from collecting officers shall be remitted daily and intact.
Disbursing and collecting functions should be segregated among different people.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should facilitate the immediate return of the unutilized balance of PDAF to the national government thru the Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the LDRRMO to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in its prescribed format as duly certified by the Municipal Accountant in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month.
We recommended to limit the utilization of the SEF to the programs and projects allowable under DepEd, DBM, and DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended and management agreed to:
Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to submit copies of perfected contracts along with the required relevant supporting documents within five working days from execution/approval.
Require the BAC through the Secretariat to submit immediately the bidding documents of the above-mentioned projects.

We recommended that the BAC should observe strictly the provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. No. 9184, specifically the prescribed timelines of the procurement activities and the required postings in the PhilGEPS to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended the following:
The Management should be judicious and frugal in its expenses in consonance with COA Circular 2012-003.
Ensure that claims for meals and snacks should be for official meetings/conferences/entertainments only and duly supported with complete documentation.
Submit attendance sheets and notice of meeting or Activity Design on the above expenses, otherwise the transaction shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended the following:
The Management should maximize the utilization of the 20% development fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development.
Additional staff should be provided to the Municipal Engineering Office whose primary responsibility is to prepare Detailed Engineering Design (DED).
Stop the practice of charging inappropriate items of expenditures against the 20% development fund, specifically those not covered under the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1. Otherwise, the transactions will be disallowed in audit.
Realign the unobligated balance of non-developmental projects totaling ₱1,896,409 to projects and programs in accordance with the guidelines set forth in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.

We recommended that the Management should maximize the utilization of the 70% of the 5% LDRRM fund to strengthen preparations for disaster risk reduction and management in compliance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

Recommendations:

Management should record the undeposited collection as part of the cash shortage of the Municipal Treasurer.








Management should demand the Municipal Treasurer to restitute the cash shortage and file appropriate legal action.


The Municipal Treasurer should be relieved from her position until full restitution of the cash shortage.

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer should have regular reconciliation of cash account. Proper monitoring of cash collection and deposit should be set up and internal control should be established to avoid future similar incident.
